zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Stewart於1250年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾時，她的父親Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland36歲，母親Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness41歲。她和Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas至少有1個兒子。她去世於1289年在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯享年39歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Stewart于1250年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾凱爾时，她的父亲Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland36岁，母亲Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness41岁。她和Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas至少有1个儿子。她去世于1289年在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯享年39岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Stewart was born in 1250, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, was 36 and her mother, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, was 41. She had at least 1 son with Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas. She died in 1289, in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, at the age of 39, and was buried in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Stewart est née en 1250, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, avait 41 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas. Elle est décédée en 1289, à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Stewart 1250 in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland 36 und ihre Mutter Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness 41. Sie hatte mit Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1289 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland und wurde in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Stewart è nata il nel 1250 nel Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, sua padre, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, aveva 41 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas. Morì nel 1289, nel Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Stewartが1250年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルで生まれた時，父Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotlandは36歳，母Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchionessは41歳であった。彼女はLord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglasとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1289年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラスで39歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Stewart(이)가 1250년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1289년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Stewart nasceu em 1250, em Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, tinha 41 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas. Ela faleceu em 1289, em Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Paisley, Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Stewart родилась в 1250 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, 41. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas. Она умерла в 1289 г. в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Stewart nació en 1250, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, tenía 41 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas. Murió en 1289, en Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Paisley, Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
